cards.text:
Characters in the Story
Mr Raven and Reynard the Fox are the main characters. The fox is clever while the raven is prideful.

Setting
The story takes place in the woods, where Mr Raven is perched on a tree limb.

The Plot
Reynard the Fox wants the morsel that Mr Raven has held tightly in his beak.

The Fox's Flattery
Reynard compliments Mr Raven, calling him a handsome bird and admiring his feathers.

The flattery's bait
  • Reynard suggests Mr Raven is so handsome that if he sang, he'd be the king of the birds.

Mr Raven's Mistake
Mr Raven tries to sing, hoping to affirm his esteemed appearance.

Revealing the Joke
Unaware, Mr Raven realizes too late the joke is on him, as he cannot sing well.

Outcome of Pride
  • Opening his beak, the morsel falls, and the Fox captures his prize.

Fox's Mockery
The Fox laughs at Mr Raven's folly and highlights his naivety.

Moral of the Story
Reynard reminds Mr Raven that pride makes him vulnerable.

Ignorance
The story emphasizes ignoring sweet, misleading words that only feed one's ego.

The Element of Surprise
For Mr Raven, the outcome serves as an unexpected lesson on pride.

Beauty vs. Talent
The story contrasts appearance (Raven's feathers) against real talent (singing).

Illusion
The Fox's flattery creates an illusion, making Mr Raven forget his own inability.

Final Lesson
Too much pride can lead to one's downfall.
